destinations.evidence: LLM pricing resolves the decoded model through read_model_from_db_cached and returns errors when output is missing, the response lacks a model, or the model is absent from the LLM registry.
The model registry reader loads model records from the ModelRegistry contract storage and reports missing contract or missing model instead of using built-in default model parameters.
destinations.snippet: pub fn read_model_from_db<DB: revm::Database>(db: &mut DB, contract: Address, model_name: &str) -> Result<Option<ModelInfo>, String> { ... return Err(format!("ModelRegistry contract not found at {contract}")); ... }

equivalence_argument: The model registry is now state-backed rather than a static once_cell table, but the invariant's fail-closed behavior is preserved: missing output, undecodable output, absent model name, or unknown registry model prevents LLM fee computation instead of falling back to heuristics/defaults.
notes: Mapped to the state-backed ModelRegistry reader used by ritual-async-fees.
rationale: The merge adds an `once_cell::sync::Lazy` model registry with explicit model entries, removes default model constants and substring heuristics, and changes missing/decode/unknown-model paths to return errors rather than fallback metrics or default parameter estimates. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: LLM executor fee computation must resolve model parameters from the static LLM model registry; if executor output is missing, undecodable, or the model cannot be resolved from registry candidates, LLM-specific pricing must fail instead of using heuristic/default model parameters.
suggested_falsification_tests: Feed LLM pricing decoded metrics for an unknown model such as `unknown/999b` and assert `compute_llm_executor_fee` returns an error rather than computing with a default parameter count; then repeat with a registered model and assert it computes using that registry entry.
summary: LLM pricing resolves model parameters through the registry and fails on unknown or undecodable model data.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call compute_llm_executor_fee with decoded metrics naming an absent model and assert it returns a registry error, then seed the registry and assert pricing uses that model info.
title: LLM pricing requires registry-known models
